# ExVerus

> ExVerus is a counterexample-guided proof-repair framework for Verus that lets large
> language models use verifier feedback to iteratively refine formal proofs. Instead of
> static, end-to-end proof generation, ExVerus learns from failed attempts by generating,
> validating, and generalizing SMT-based counterexamples into inductive loop invariants.

**Paper:** ExVerus: Verus Proof Repair via Counterexample Reasoning (ICML 2026)
**Authors:** Jun Yang, Yuechun Sun, Yi Wu, Rodrigo Caridad (University of Chicago);
Yongwei Yuan (Purdue University); Jianan Yao (University of Toronto);
Shan Lu (University of Chicago, Microsoft Research); Kexin Pei (University of Chicago).

## How ExVerus works

ExVerus transforms static proof generation into an iterative reasoning process with three phases:

1. **Initial Generation** — produce an initial proof with a baseline LLM prompt; failed proofs trigger repair.
2. **Counterexample Generation** — generate and validate SMT-based counterexamples that reveal real invariant failures.
3. **Generalization & Repair** — classify errors, mutate invariants to block the counterexamples, and select the best repair via blocking validation.

## Key results

- State-of-the-art verification success on VerusBench and DafnyBench (up to 95.5% with O4-mini); solves more tasks on HumanEval and LCBench. Roughly +45% over prior SOTA.
- Robustness: on ObfsBench (266 obfuscated Verus tasks covering layout, data, and control-flow changes), 90% of ExVerus-verified proofs remain verifiable, vs. 33%–50% for AutoVerus.
- Efficiency: ~75% fewer tokens than AutoVerus on DeepSeek and ~25% fewer on GPT-4o.

## Links

- Website: https://exverus-proof.github.io/
- Paper (PDF): https://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.25810
- OpenReview: https://openreview.net/forum?id=FNDkXA0OUJ

## Topics

formal verification, Verus, Rust, large language models, automated proof repair,
counterexample-guided invariant synthesis, inductive loop invariants, SMT solving, code generation
